question: What should I check on the submittals page?

response.answer:
On the Submittals page, check the submittal log for:

- Number and revision
- Spec section and title
- Type
- Responsible contractor
- Submittal coordinator
- Reviewer
- Status and due dates

Documented behavior says this page is for managing the submittal log, review workflow, and revision tracking.

Key workflow to check:
- Whether the submittal has been submitted to the coordinator
- Whether it has been forwarded to the reviewer
- Whether it was returned, approved, or closed
- Whether a “Revise and Resubmit” item has a new revision and restarted review

Documented review outcomes:
- Approved (No Exceptions Taken)
- Approved as Noted
- Revise and Resubmit
- Rejected
- For Information Only

Useful routes and sources:
- Main page: `/[projectId]/submittals`
- Help article: `docs/help/articles/submittals.md`
- Implementation-backed page route: `frontend/src/app/(main)/[projectId]/submittals/page.tsx`

What’s still uncertain:
- I did not find a help article linked to the detail route `/[projectId]/submittals/[submittalId]` or edit route `/[projectId]/submittals/[submittalId]/edit`, so any page-specific behavior there is implementation evidence only.

toolTrace.detail: App Expert produced an answer from read-only app knowledge tools.
